summary.headline: ADM settles SEC investigation into intersegment sales with $40M penalty and DOJ closes its investigation.
summary.narrative_summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Company announced it reached a settlement with the SEC resolving its investigation into intersegment sales without admitting or denying any wrongdoing. The company agreed to pay a $40 million civil penalty. The Department of Justice also closed its investigation with no further action. The settlement concludes both investigations and follows corrections to prior period financial statements related to segment reporting.

regulatory_or_policy_mentions: Sections 10(b), 13(a), 13(b)(2)(A), and 13(b)(2)(B) of the Exchange Act

leadership_changes.name: Juan Luciano
leadership_changes.role: Chair of the Board, President and CEO

ma_or_capital_allocation.description: Civil penalty payment to SEC in settlement of investigation.

extraction_notes: Numbers and details extracted directly from the press release and cover sheet; no financial figures or segment performance data provided.
